methodsAttempted.query: site:vu.nl ("generatieve AI" OR "generative AI" OR ChatGPT) (studenten OR onderwijs OR toetsing OR assessment) "Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official VU student guidance, framework news, a teacher didactics page, and an event page.
Dutch search exposed the original-language framework news page and framework PDF.
Follow-up official-domain search found the VU Research and AI page.
Related Digital Student Guide paths identified official Cogniti and VU Education Lab AI Assistant tool pages.
The official VU news page linked the April 2025 Dutch framework PDF hosted on VU's content asset domain.

artifacts.summary: Official VU Amsterdam sources were found on vu.nl and VU's official content asset host. Claims use student guidance, the Dutch framework PDF, teacher guidance, research guidance, academic-integrity guidance, and Cogniti tool guidance. Event/news-only and research-showcase sources were rejected as evidence.

targets.sourceTitle: Kader voor generatieve AI in het onderwijs
How to deal with ChatGPT and Microsoft Copilot as a teacher

stopConditions: Stop after official VU sources directly supporting generative-AI education, student-use, privacy, research, and approved-tool claims are fetched.
Do not use third-party news, search snippets, event listings, or research-showcase papers as claim evidence.
Do not bypass Canvas login-gated pages or other access controls.

artifacts.claimText: VU Amsterdam's April 2025 Dutch framework describes the university's vision and policy for generative AI in education and gives faculties conditions for formulating their own faculty AI education policy.

citation.citationTitle: Kader voor generatieve AI in het onderwijs
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Kader voor generatieve AI in het onderwijs
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Dit document verwoordt de visie en beleid van de Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam (VU) op het gebruik van generatieve AI in het onderwijs en geeft faculteiten randvoorwaarden om hun eigen facultaire AI-onderwijsbeleid te formuleren.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim is limited to the framework's stated scope and faculty-policy function.

artifacts.claimText: VU Amsterdam's framework says the lecturer or examiner indicates in the study guide, syllabus, or Canvas whether and how use of generative AI is allowed for a course, with course learning objectives remaining leading.

citation.citationTitle: Kader voor generatieve AI in het onderwijs

artifacts.sourceTitle: Kader voor generatieve AI in het onderwijs
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: De docent of examinator geeft in de studiehandleiding, syllabus of Canvas aan of en hoe het gebruik van (gen)AI is toegestaan. Daarbij geldt dat de leerdoelen van het vak te allen tijde leidend zijn.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim follows the framework wording and does not infer a university-wide default beyond course-level permission.

artifacts.claimText: VU Amsterdam's framework says students cannot be required to use AI tools for which VU has no license, and students, lecturers, and support staff do not process personal data or other confidential data in AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Kader voor generatieve AI in het onderwijs
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Kader voor generatieve AI in het onderwijs
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Een student kan niet verplicht worden om van AI-tools gebruik te maken waar de VU geen licentie voor heeft. Studenten, docenten en ondersteunend personeel verwerken geen persoonsgegevens of andere vertrouwelijke gegevens in AI-tools vanwege de risico’s met betrekking tot gegevensbescherming.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: PDF page 7, section 4 Kaders voor het gebruik van (gen)AI in onderwijs

artifacts.claimText: VU Amsterdam's Dutch academic-integrity page states that work made by generative AI is not the student's own work and lists using an AI writer to write an assignment as an example of fraud.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: VU Amsterdam's student guidance states that Microsoft Copilot is available to VU students and staff through VU credentials, that teachers cannot force ChatGPT use, and that teachers may ask students to use Microsoft Copilot via the VU license.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Since February 2024, Microsoft Copilot is available to VU students and staff. To use this chatbot securely, log in with your VU credentials. Teachers cannot force you to use ChatGPT, but can in fact ask you to use Microsoft Copilot for assignments via the VU license.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim is confined to the availability and course-use wording on the official student page.

artifacts.claimText: VU Amsterdam's research guidance says GenAI use in research must meet scientific-research standards, take existing VU policies on ownership, scientific integrity, privacy, personal data, and knowledge security into account, and be transparent about which parts of research used GenAI.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of GenAI must meet the standards of scientific research. Research must be truthful, valid, original and reproducible. And existing VU policies around ownership, scientific integrity, privacy, personal data, and knowledge security must be taken into account. Transparency: Be transparent, clearly indicate which parts of your research were done using GenAI.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Research and AI page, Responsibilities section
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim directly combines adjacent responsibility bullets from the official research guidance page.

artifacts.claimText: VU Amsterdam's Cogniti page describes Cogniti as an online-learning platform offered by VU where teachers or staff can create generative AI Agents for students, integrated into Canvas or used stand-alone at edubots.vu.nl in some cases.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Cogniti is an additional platform for online learning that Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam offers. With Cogniti, teachers can create Agents (AI bots) that support teaching with interactive learning opportunities based on generative AI. At VU Amsterdam, Cogniti is integrated into the Learning Management System (Canvas).
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim is limited to the official Cogniti product description and access context.

artifacts.title: Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam generative AI policy source review
artifacts.summary: Official VU Amsterdam sources support staged claims on the April 2025 generative-AI education framework, course-level AI-use permission, privacy and licensed-tool boundaries, academic integrity and AI writers, AI detector non-use, Copilot availability through VU credentials, research GenAI responsibilities, and Cogniti as a VU-offered generative-AI learning platform.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged bundle is not a canonical public record and sets neither publishAsCanonical nor isCanonical.
Canvas-hosted materials linked from official pages were not used when they were not publicly accessible during this crawl.
Search snippets were used only for discovery; claim evidence comes from fetched official VU sources.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
